title: Freshly Fried German Boudin Noir Tart
description: A fresh and gooey German dish featuring boudin noir, perfect for midnight cravings, this tart is made with a flaky crust and a rich, savory filling.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, salt, and pepper.
Add the cold butter to the bowl and use a pastry blender to work it into the flour mixture until it resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually add the eggs to the bowl, stirring with a fork until the dough comes together.
Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead it a few times until it becomes smooth and pliable.
Roll the dough out into a thin sheet, about 1/8 inch thick.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a tablespoon of boudin noir in the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the filling, forming a triangle or a square shape, and press the edges together to seal the tart.
Heat the vegetable oil in a deep frying pan over medium high heat.
Fry the tarts until they are golden brown, about 2-3 minutes on each side.
